title: tagetes perezii
intro: Tagetes perezii, also known as the Mexican marigold or zempasúchil, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Mexico and Guatemala. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of terpenoids and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Tagetes perezii have been reported to have various health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and antiviral activity, as well as potential antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal use, and its flowers are also used in traditional ceremonies and as a natural dye.
